topic.what: Constant-time code verification to prevent timing side-channel attacks
topic.how: Dynamic analysis of low-level instruction traces comparing instruction mix vectors across secret inputs to detect deviations from constant-time behavior
topic.why_matters: Timing side-channel attacks can extract secret keys from cryptographic implementations; existing verification methods (formal abstractions, timing measurements) have fundamental limitations in capturing real execution behavior

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to correctly identify constant-time vs non-constant-time programs
characteristics.evidence: Evaluated on 22 known constant-time and non-constant-time examples; perfect detection across all cases
characteristics.context: Examples from ct-tools repository plus AES, modular exponentiation, password checking implementations

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of execution rounds to detect constant-time violations
characteristics.evidence: Using havoc-based fuzzing, no benchmark required more than 5 runs
characteristics.context: Lightweight detection achievable with minimal iterations

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of instruction classes used for analysis
characteristics.evidence: Memory (load/store) × data types (int/float/vector) + ALU light/heavy × data types + control-flow
characteristics.context: Covers all performance-relevant distinctions without per-instruction overhead

characteristics.evidence: Tested across GCC and Clang at -O0, -O1, -O2 optimization levels with consistent results
characteristics.context: Independent of compiler optimizations and compilation flags

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to detect hardware-level side channels like cache timing
characteristics.evidence: Does not model cache behavior; cannot detect prime+probe or flush+reload attacks
characteristics.context: Logical execution only; microarchitectural state not captured

good_for: verifying constant-time property of cryptographic binaries
detecting secret-dependent control flow in compiled programs
not_for: exhaustive path coverage without diverse inputs

novelty: instruction-mix-vector approach using 13 instruction classes (type × data-type)
trace equivalence-based constant-time formalization using Manhattan norm
light ALU vs heavy ALU latency classification
compiler/optimization/architecture agnostic verification at binary level
open_problems: integration with fuzzing or symbolic execution for comprehensive input coverage
extending to additional ISAs beyond x86/ARM
